task_completion.description: Verify the Sample Order Lines dataset is loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Sample Order Lines' or 'sample_order_lines2023' in the Data Elements panel on the left side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify a Grid Heat Map visualization has been created on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a grid-style heatmap chart on the main canvas workspace.

task_completion.description: Verify that Month, Day of Week, and Sales are used in the heatmap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Grammar panel or the chart axes/legend. 'Month' and 'Day of Week' should be assigned to the Category/Rows/Columns, and 'Sales' should be assigned to Color.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Month and Day of Week axes are sorted chronologically.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the labels on the heatmap axes. Months should appear in order (e.g., Jan, Feb, Mar...) and Days of Week in order (e.g., Sun, Mon, Tue...), NOT alphabetically (which would be Apr, Aug, Dec... or Fri, Mon, Sat...).

task_completion.description: Verify the canvas has been renamed to 'Staffing Analysis'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the canvas tab (usually at the bottom of the workspace); it should read 'Staffing Analysis' instead of the default 'Canvas 1'.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved as 'Seasonal_Patterns.dva' in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the top title bar of the application for 'Seasonal_Patterns', or look for a save dialog/file explorer window showing 'Seasonal_Patterns.dva' located in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface with its standard menus, data panels, and grammar panes, not a terminal window or Python script generating a plot.

integrity.description: Verify the heatmap is a genuine Oracle Analytics visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: The heatmap should be rendered within the application's canvas area with interactive elements visible (like hover tooltips or selection boxes), not an imported static image.

integrity.description: Verify the heatmap reflects actual dataset values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The heatmap cells should show varying colors/intensities corresponding to the Sales data, rather than a uniform grid or manually drawn shapes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'Seasonal_Patterns.dva' (saved in the Documents folder). The canvas must be renamed to 'Staffing Analysis'. The visualization must be a Grid Heat Map containing 'Month', 'Day of Week', and 'Sales' columns. Note that Month and Day of Week must be derived from Order Date and sorted chronologically (e.g., Jan, Feb, Mar... and Mon, Tue, Wed...) rather than alphabetically.
